provenance.notes: Supply counts are contract-derived. Materialized rescue/history bucket membership lives in data/rescue-buckets.json. Calendar-year and first-week/day bucket method notes record UTC rescue-time boundary evidence from a user-provided MoonCat Discord FAQ note; the four cutoff transaction timestamps were independently checked against public Etherscan transaction page metadata on 2026-07-03. Rescue-order indexes are not token IDs or bytes5 catIds.

ranges.label: Normal rescue cat supply
ranges.criteria: Initial remainingCats value in the contract source: 25600 - 256.
ranges.notes: This is a supply count, not a rescue-order range or list of IDs.

ranges.label: Genesis cat supply cap
ranges.criteria: Initial remainingGenesisCats value in the contract source.
ranges.notes: This is a maximum/supply cap, not a list of Genesis IDs.

ranges.label: Total MoonCat supply
ranges.criteria: totalSupply value in the contract source.
ranges.notes: Normal rescue supply plus Genesis supply cap.

fields.label: Human-readable range name.
fields.basis: Whether the entry is based on contract constants, rescue order, block, timestamp, ID, or derived chain data.

todos: Verify rescue-order cutoff numbers or event context separately before treating the Discord FAQ ranges as fully canonical-onchain derivations.
Define early-rescue ranges separately by rescue order, block, timestamp, or community convention where needed beyond existing bucket arrays.
Define any conversion from rescue-order-index to catId/token-facing identifiers before downstream use.
Keep materialized bucket arrays in data/rescue-buckets.json rather than duplicating them here.

bucketBoundaryMethod.basis: UTC rescue-time boundaries from user-provided MoonCat Discord FAQ note
limitations: This records method evidence for existing bucket arrays; it does not duplicate memberships from data/rescue-buckets.json.
This pass verified the four cutoff transaction timestamp strings against public Etherscan transaction page metadata.
This pass did not import event logs or independently derive rescue-order numbers from chain data.
Use data/identifier-conventions.json before converting rescue-order indexes to other identifier spaces.
